WTI.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $89.83 (-8.1%) vs history prev_close $94.69 (-12.8%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

Gold.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $4879.60 (-1.4%) vs history prev_close $4785.40 (+0.6%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: Markets rallied Friday as Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' to commercial shipping for the duration of the ceasefire, per BBC reporting. The S&P 500 closed up 1.2% and SPY finished at $710.14 (+1.12%), with European indices gaining 2% on the news. VIX fell to 17.48 (-2.56%), continuing its compression streak to multi-week lows. The Hormuz opening is a material de-escalation — tanker traffic had slowed to a trickle since late February — though maritime group BIMCO cautioned operators that mine threat status in the traffic separation scheme remains unclear and the area should not yet be considered safe for transit, per their official advisory.
commodities_note: WTI crude has a data anomaly: the fast_info source shows a prior close of $89.83 (implying an -8.1% decline to $82.59), while history data shows a prior close of $94.69 (implying -12.8%). Using the fast_info headline of $82.59 (-8.1%) as the canonical figure, but the true daily move is uncertain — the directional signal is clear (oil dropped sharply on Hormuz opening) but the exact magnitude is unreliable. Context: Brent crude fell to $88/bbl from above $98 intraday on Iran's announcement, per BBC. Oil has now retraced nearly the entire war premium — it was below $70/bbl before the conflict. Gold also shows a data anomaly: fast_info implies $4,813.30 (-1.36% from $4,879.60) while history implies +0.6% from $4,785.40. Using fast_info as headline; the directional read is a modest pullback from near all-time highs. Despite the oil plunge, gold remains elevated above $4,800 — its strength is now decoupled from the Hormuz story and appears driven by USD weakness (DXY 98.3) and macro uncertainty. US10Y eased to 4.25% (-1.46%), moving closer to the 4.20% threshold that would ease NIM pressure on Singapore banks. Gold at ATH territory with GLD extended well above its 50-day average — no attractive entry exists at current levels.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. DBS is the cleanest setup in the Singapore cluster right now — sitting 1.67% above its 50-day average of SGD 56.37 with RSI at 58, neither overbought nor oversold. The Hormuz opening and US10Y easing to 4.25% are moving in the right direction: the 4.20% reset threshold that would ease net interest margin compression is now just 5 basis points away. Volume has not confirmed the recent recovery (below average), so sizing should reflect that. The invalidation is the 50-day average at SGD 56.37 — a close below that confirms the rally has failed. Nearest resistance is thin at SGD 57.47 (just 0.3% away), with the more meaningful ceiling at SGD 58.78. The headline R:R to SGD 58.78 is 1.6:1, which falls short of the 2.0 threshold for ACCUMULATE — and the tight stop means normal intraday volatility could trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested; use SGD 52.79 (200-day SMA) as the mental stop for position sizing, where the sizing R:R drops to 0.3:1. At 13.8x forward earnings with a 5.34% yield, DBS is fairly valued relative to its cluster peers. Earnings decline of -8.8% expected by analysts is a mild headwind, not a thesis-breaker. The one trigger that upgrades this to a stronger signal: US10Y closing below 4.20% on a sustained basis, which would remove the structural NIM headwind that has dogged the Singapore bank cluster for 36+ days. Bottom line: DBS offers a decent yield at a fair price, but wait for US10Y to break below 4.20% before committing — that's the one number that changes everything here.

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. OCBC is 5.3% above its 50-day average with RSI at 65.7 — the pipeline hard-blocks entry here, and that block is correct given the extension and overbought momentum. Per Retail Banker International (April 17), OCBC is reportedly seeking to acquire HSBC's consumer banking business in Indonesia, potentially valued at more than Rp6tn (~$350M) — a deal that would expand Southeast Asian retail exposure but adds integration risk and capital deployment uncertainty at an already stretched technical level. The 1.0:1 R:R (derived levels, not observed swing points) offers no margin of safety. The rising 50-day average is constructive longer-term, but 19 days above the SMA without a consolidation at RSI 66 creates profit-taking vulnerability. Would reconsider above a confirmed Hormuz-driven NIM reset (US10Y below 4.20%) and a pullback to the SGD 22.16 support zone. Avoid at current levels.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from ACCUMULATE to WATCH — the rr_distorted flag is true, blocking ACCUMULATE re-assignment today. UOB sits just 0.6% above its 50-day average of SGD 37.21, which is the tightest stop on the watchlist. The headline R:R of 9.2:1 to the SGD 39.49 resistance is mathematically distorted by that near-zero denominator — a 0.6% stop means normal intraday volatility will trigger invalidation before the thesis is meaningfully tested. The actionable sizing R:R using the SGD 35.92 structural support is only 1.4:1, which also falls below the 2.0 threshold required for ACCUMULATE. Tight stop disclosure: use SGD 35.92 as the mental stop for position sizing, not SGD 37.21. UOB trades at a 18.1% discount to the Singapore cluster median PE of 12.7x (forward PE 10.4x) with a 3.8% yield — the valuation is unambiguously cheap relative to peers. The PEG of 1.01 looks reasonable, though analyst consensus expects a -6.7% earnings decline, which tempers enthusiasm. With US10Y at 4.25% and approaching the 4.20% NIM reset threshold, UOB is one rate tick away from a re-rating catalyst. No earnings for 17 days (May 7, approximate). Bottom line: UOB is cheap and getting close to a setup — the one thing missing is the US10Y breaking 4.20% cleanly, which would unlock the NIM thesis and make the entry much cleaner.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — NVDA has surged 10% above its 50-day average of $183.29 with RSI at 71.4, but the prior CAUTION was driven by duration headwinds that are now partially easing (US10Y falling to 4.25%, VIX at 17.48). NVDA's quantum computing catalyst play is live: per Insider Monkey reporting, NVIDIA unveiled two new quantum-focused products this week, sending XNDU up 237% and IONQ up 60% — this is narrative momentum, not a direct NVDA revenue driver in the near term. The core Blackwell Ultra thesis (Q4 FY2026 revenue $68.1B, Q1 FY2027 guide $78B per NVDA earnings call) remains intact, but at $201.68 the risk/reward is unfavorable — 9.1% downside to the 50-day average versus 5.2% upside to the $212.17 resistance, a 0.6:1 ratio. Support zones at $177.41 and $169.81 are the meaningful levels to watch on any pullback. At 17.9x forward earnings with PEG 0.72 and 73% revenue growth, NVDA is genuinely cheap for its growth rate — but the technical extension means the entry window is not now. A pullback toward $183-188 with volume confirmation would reopen the setup. Hold; no new entry at current levels.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. AMD has gone parabolic — up 31% above its 50-day average of $212.13 with RSI at 80.2, placing it in extreme extension territory with no valid entry. The hard block is correct. The Meta GPU supply framework (up to 6GW of MI450 Instinct, per AMD IR February 24, 2026) remains the load-bearing thesis, but per the standing guardrails, this is a framework ceiling, not a firm purchase order — revenue is contingent on MI450 benchmark performance, with first 1GW shipments not until H2 2026. Bank of America recently revised its price target per TheStreet (April 18), reflecting the 42% one-month run. At $278.39, the downside to the 50-day SMA alone is 23.8% — the R:R from here is 0.2:1, making any new entry indefensible. FCF yield is thin at 1.01% and the trailing PE of 106x reflects the speculative premium being paid. AMD earnings are due approximately May 6 (16 days, approximate). Wait for a meaningful pullback toward the $224 support zone or SMA50 convergence before reconsidering. No entry at current levels.

INTC.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 28% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Intel has surged 52% in a month and sits nearly 40% above its 50-day average — extreme extension by any measure, hard-blocked from entry. The catalyst driving this move is the Google AI and cloud collaboration plus participation in Elon Musk's Terafab project, per Simply Wall St. (April 19). However, per standing thesis guardrails, Intel's 18A node has no confirmed external customers as of April 2026 — internal products (Panther Lake) only. The Terafab and Google deals may be real, but the foundry thesis remains unproven. Intel earnings arrive in approximately 4 days (April 24, per calendar) — a binary event that makes any entry now doubly inadvisable. At 64.1x forward PE with negative FCF yield (-1.31%) and negative revenue growth (-4.1%), Intel trades at a 168% premium to the Semis cluster median PE of 23.9x with no fundamental justification visible yet. The SMA50 at $48.99 is 28% below current price — a pullback to that level would be a crash, not a normal correction. Use $71.92 resistance and $78.77 as near-term reference levels. Wait for post-earnings clarity and significant technical normalization. No entry.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from BUY to WATCH — ASML's price has run 4.36% above its 50-day average of $1,398.82, crossing the 2-5% 'slightly extended' zone without volume confirmation. Q1 results were strong (EPS EUR 7.15 vs EUR 6.00 a year ago, revenue EUR 8.77B vs EUR 7.74B, per Insider Monkey April 19), and ASML raised its outlook on AI demand — but the stock's inability to hold gains post-earnings (5d return -1.25%) signals that good news was priced in. The China angle adds friction: per Tech in Asia, Chinese chipmakers including SMIC are retrofitting older ASML DUV machines to boost AI chip output, raising compliance and export restriction complexity. R:R at current levels is 0.5:1 — $1,491.51 upside target versus $1,398.82 invalidation. This is not a favorable entry. At 30.6x forward PE with PEG 2.23, ASML is expensive relative to its 13.2% revenue growth rate; the growth-to-multiple ratio flags valuation concern even if the AI capex thesis is intact. The High-NA EUV ramp for Intel 14A in H2 2026 remains the key forward catalyst. A pullback to the $1,382-$1,399 zone (50-day SMA support) with volume would restore the BUY setup. Bottom line: ASML is the right company but the wrong entry point — it's expensive for what you're getting right now, and the post-earnings fade suggests the market agrees.

MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Micron sits 12% above its 50-day average of $406.30, hard-blocked from entry, with RSI at 62.6. The valuation case remains compelling on paper — 4.5x forward PE and analyst consensus expects 756% earnings growth — but the PEG of 0.26 alongside a forward PE of 4.5x is anomalous and should be treated with caution rather than cited as a primary argument; verify the earnings growth estimate before acting on it. The nearest resistance is essentially at the current price ($455.31, just 0.1% above), meaning the stock needs to clear that level convincingly before the $471 target becomes meaningful. The critical new development: per Simply Wall St. (April 19), updated US export controls on advanced wafer-fab tools and services to certain Chinese customers are introducing fresh uncertainty for Micron's supply chain and AI memory outlook. This is a real, specific risk — not generic trade war noise — as China is a meaningful memory market. Per standing thesis guardrails, distinguish inference-driven HBM demand (KV cache) from training demand; inference remains the structural driver. The FCF yield of 0.56% means the thesis depends entirely on earnings growth materializing. Wait for a pullback to the $406-$420 zone before re-evaluating entry. No new position at current levels.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

TSM.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at BUY. TSM is the cleanest actionable entry on the watchlist — 4.74% above its rising 50-day average of $353.74 with RSI at 58.6 (neutral zone) and volume confirmed. Q1 earnings showed 58% profit growth, per Barchart (April 19), directly validating the AI chip demand thesis. The post-earnings price action has been muted (5d return -0.03%), which is actually constructive — it suggests the market is digesting strong results rather than selling the news aggressively. At 19.3x forward PE with PEG 1.23 and 35% revenue growth, TSM trades at a 19% discount to the Semis cluster median PE (23.9x) — you are getting the physical bottleneck of the entire AI buildout at a below-peer-average multiple. The invalidation is a close below $353.74 (50-day SMA), which would confirm rally failure. Upside target $378.93 (nearest resistance, 2.3% above). The R:R of 0.5:1 to the near term target is low, but the structural support at $330.99 (10.7% below) provides a larger buffer for patient holders. NVDA has booked 50%+ of CoWoS capacity; Amazon, Meta, and Anthropic demand signals (Motley Fool, April 19) are all additive. Volume confirmation is a green flag absent on most other watchlist names. Entering slightly above the 50-day average is the trade-off for the volume confirmation. Bottom line: TSM is the AI infrastructure pick that's actually trading at a reasonable price — the profit growth is real, the demand is diversified across customers, and the entry is clean right now.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Amazon is 16.9% above its declining 50-day average of $214.24 with RSI at 76.6 — hard-blocked, overbought, and with a 0.2:1 R:R. The $200B AI capex commitment (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings) remains the structural driver, but when does that translate to earnings? Analyst consensus expects only 5% earnings growth, a thin return on a $200B bet. The Blue Origin New Glenn rocket failed to deploy the AST SpaceMobile satellite into the correct orbit on Sunday, per Reuters — a setback for the space initiative but not material to the core AWS/advertising thesis. AMZN earnings are due approximately April 30 (10 days) — a binary event that further argues against entering now. At 26.6x forward PE with PEG 1.81, AMZN is fairly priced for 13.6% revenue growth but not cheap. The structural support from the 200-day SMA at $225.58 is the level to watch if the post-earnings reaction disappoints. No entry until either a pullback to the $225-$226 support zone or post-earnings clarity on AWS revenue acceleration.

The nearest resistance at $340.96 is just 0.5% away, with the next meaningful level at $349.90. The 200-day SMA at $272.55 is the structural support far below. Wait for post-earnings re-set or a pullback to the $308-$319 zone. No entry now.

Wait for either a pullback to $391-$395 or a post-earnings reset.

AVGO.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Broadcom is 22% above its 50-day average of $333 — extreme extension, hard-blocked, RSI at 79.1. Per Insider Monkey (April 18), Reuters reported that Meta has expanded its partnership with AVGO to develop several generations of custom AI processors through 2029 — a multi-year deal that validates AVGO's custom ASIC dominance (Google TPU, Meta training, OpenAI/ByteDance inference). AVGO earnings are 45 days out (June 4), removing earnings-proximity risk but also removing a near-term catalyst. No entry at current levels.

PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from ACCUMULATE to WATCH — the 5-day momentum block (+14.3%) prevents ACCUMULATE assignment today, and the prior ACCUMULATE conditions have changed. PLTR is now 2.05% above its 50-day average of $143.45 with RSI at 52.2 — the technical setup is actually improving (RSI cooling from prior overbought levels), but the momentum chase block is a hard rule. The defense AI contract thesis is strong: the $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling (not firm revenue) plus the $448M Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) provide the demand backdrop, and 2026 revenue guidance of $7.18-7.20B (+61% YoY) is compelling. Earnings are due approximately May 5 (15 days) — Motley Fool predicts a 'huge move' on that date, which is binary risk that argues for waiting. The R:R to the $155.98 resistance (using the next meaningful zone past the tight $148.21 level) is 3.3:1 from the $143.45 invalidation — favorable, but the nearest resistance at $148.21 is only 1.2% away, meaning the pipeline substituted the next target at $155.98 as the meaningful ceiling. At 78.6x forward PE with PEG 2.89, PLTR is deeply expensive for even its 70% revenue growth — the valuation is a perpetual caveat. Support at $145.06 is the first line of defense. If the stock consolidates for 2-3 more sessions and the momentum block clears, this becomes the strongest WATCH-to-ACCUMULATE candidate on the list. Bottom line: PLTR has the right thesis and is approaching a clean setup — wait for the momentum to cool and for post-earnings clarity before entering.

singapore.summary: The Hormuz opening and US10Y sliding to 4.25% are the most positive developments for Singapore banks in weeks — the NIM reset threshold of 4.20% is now just 5 basis points away. DBS (WATCH) is the only actionable name; OCBC (CAUTION) is technically blocked at 5.3% extension with RSI near overbought, and UOB (WATCH) has a distorted R:R from a near-zero stop. The cluster stance remains cautiously constructive but action is premature until US10Y confirms a sustained break below 4.20%.
singapore.thesis_status: Approaching reset threshold — monitor US10Y closely
singapore.key_development: US10Y at 4.25% puts the Singapore bank NIM reset trigger (4.20%) within striking distance for the first time in 36 days of compression.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX +2.32% to $415.71 extends the semis recovery, with TSM the standout — 58% Q1 profit growth confirmed and volume confirming the move while most peers run extended and volume-less. ASML downgraded from BUY to WATCH after failing to hold post-earnings gains. AMD, INTC, NVDA, AVGO, and LITE are all in extreme extension territory with no valid entries. MU's export control risk adds friction to an otherwise strong earnings setup. The cluster is bullish in trend but offers only one clean entry (TSM).
semiconductors.key_development: ASML post-earnings fade (5d -1.25% despite strong Q1) signals AI capex optimism is priced into the sector — TSM's volume-confirmed holding is the better tell on genuine demand.

big_tech.summary: The BigTech cluster faces a critical week: AMZN, GOOG, and MSFT all report earnings around April 30, and all three are extended with hard entry blocks. PLTR is the most interesting setup — cooling from ACCUMULATE to WATCH on the momentum block, approaching a cleaner entry with earnings May 5. The cluster divergence within BigTech is valuation-driven: MSFT at 22.4x forward PE is the cheapest; PLTR at 78.6x is the most speculative.
big_tech.thesis_status: Earnings-week positioning — all names extended, await reset
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's Fairwater data center going live ahead of schedule adds Azure supply capacity, but the April 30 earnings call (focus: non-OpenAI Azure RPO) is the real test of whether the AI revenue thesis is organic.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp (COHR)
interconnected.reason: NVIDIA's matching $2B strategic investment (same March 2, 2026 announcement as LITE) makes COHR the direct photonics peer to LITE; hyperscaler datacenter AI spending directly drives both optics suppliers.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels or if LITE holds $850+ for 5 sessions, confirming photonics sector durability.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology (MRVL)
interconnected.reason: Investor's Business Daily (April 19) reports Google is in talks with Marvell for new TPU generations — a direct AVGO custom ASIC competitor dynamic that links MRVL to GOOG capex and TSM fabrication capacity.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes watchlist-worthy if Google formally confirms a TPU design win; revisit after GOOG earnings April 30 for any custom silicon commentary.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (2330.TW)
interconnected.reason: TSM's CoWoS packaging capacity is booked above 50% by NVDA; Q1 58% profit growth confirms AI chip demand is translating to foundry revenue — the same supply signal that supports ASML's EUV order pipeline.
interconnected.entry_note: Already tracked as TSM (US ADR) on the watchlist; the Taiwan-listed ADR is only relevant if Marcus gains direct access to the Taiwan Stock Exchange — no separate action needed.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix (000660.KS)
interconnected.reason: Primary HBM memory competitor to MU; if US export controls constrain MU's China supply chain (per Simply Wall St., April 19), SK Hynix gains share in the HBM market serving NVDA Blackwell and other AI accelerators.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MU's next earnings (approximate date TBD) includes guidance cut on China revenue — that's the direct share-shift trigger for SK Hynix.

interconnected.name: Palantir-adjacent defense: CACI International (CACI)
interconnected.reason: As PLTR's defense AI contract momentum builds (Army IDIQ + Navy ShipOS), CACI operates in the same government IT/AI services space and would benefit from the same Pentagon AI spending cycle.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit CACI if PLTR's May 5 earnings confirm defense revenue acceleration — a PLTR beat would validate the government AI spending cycle that CACI competes in.

active_risks: Iran ceasefire active — Hormuz declared 'open' but BIMCO warns mine threat status unclear; tanker transit not yet certified safe
US-China tech war: export controls on advanced wafer-fab tools tightened, affecting Micron's China supply chain and ASML's DUV compliance exposure
Q1 earnings season for BigTech (AMZN, GOOG, MSFT ~April 30) — hyperscaler capex guidance is the key AI cycle validator
US tariff regime: Section 122 surcharges (15%, 150 days) active; China 34% retaliatory tariffs + rare earths export controls ongoing
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz opening sent oil down sharply (WTI ~$82.59) and lifted equities broadly, with VIX compressing to 17.48 — the war risk premium is unwinding but BIMCO's mine-threat advisory keeps logistics costs elevated, which continues to pressure TSM and ASML supply chains. US10Y at 4.25% is approaching the 4.20% level where Singapore bank NIM pressure eases; PLTR remains the primary watchlist beneficiary of the defense AI spending cycle that the Iran conflict accelerated.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran's Foreign Minister declared the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' for commercial vessels during the ceasefire, per BBC; oil fell to ~$82-88/bbl range. BIMCO separately cautioned operators about unresolved mine threats in the traffic separation scheme.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Selective entry on quality names at clean technical setups (TSM, DBS approaching), avoid chasing extended tech; the Hormuz de-escalation removes the acute tail risk but doesn't resolve US-China tech restrictions or the tariff backdrop.

events_this_week.event: Amazon (AMZN) earnings — AWS revenue growth and $200B capex ROI commentary critical
Alphabet (GOOG) earnings — TPU demand, Cloud growth, and AI monetization update
Microsoft (MSFT) earnings — focus on non-OpenAI Azure RPO growth and Fairwater capacity ramp
DBS Group (D05.SI) earnings — NIM trajectory and rate sensitivity commentary
Non-Farm Payrolls (April) — 08:30 ET; labor market health check for Fed rate path
Palantir (PLTR) earnings — defense contract revenue and 2026 guidance validation; Motley Fool flags potential large price move
AMD earnings — MI450 ramp timeline and Meta framework order progress

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously constructive — Hormuz de-escalation shifts tone, but most names technically extended
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Nearly all high-conviction names (AMD, NVDA, INTC, AVGO, AMZN, GOOG) are 10-40% above their 50-day averages with hard entry blocks, leaving TSM as the sole clean BUY and the Singapore banks as the emerging opportunity if US10Y breaks 4.20%.

consider_adding.note: 58% Q1 profit growth confirmed, volume supporting the move, AI chip demand diversified across NVDA/AMD/Google/Meta. Half position — entry slightly above 50-day average, invalidation at $353.74.

watch_for_entry.note: Closest to a clean setup in the Singapore cluster. Actionable if US10Y closes below 4.20% on a sustained basis — that's the NIM reset trigger. Stop below SGD 56.37.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter SGD 56.50-57.00 on US10Y 4.20% break, stop below SGD 56.37

watch_for_entry.note: Cheapest Singapore bank at 10.4x forward PE, 18% discount to cluster. Actionable when US10Y breaks 4.20% — use SGD 35.92 as the mental stop, not the 50-day SMA.
Momentum block clears in 2-3 sessions if price consolidates. Earnings May 5 are the binary catalyst — wait for post-earnings reset unless block clears with RSI below 55. Stop below SGD 143.45.
Post-earnings fade creates re-entry opportunity. Actionable on pullback to $1,382-$1,399 (50-day SMA) with volume. High-NA EUV ramp H2 2026 is the forward catalyst.

on_deck.note: 17.9x forward PE with 73% revenue growth and PEG 0.72 — genuinely cheap for its growth rate. Technically blocked at 10% extension with RSI 71.
on_deck.rationale: Blackwell Ultra ramp intact, quantum computing narrative adding incremental attention. Core AI infrastructure thesis unbroken.
on_deck.trigger: Price pulls back to $183-188 (50-day SMA zone) with RSI cooling below 55 and volume confirming support

on_deck.note: 4.5x forward PE and inference-driven HBM demand structural. Technically blocked at 12% extension. Export control risk is the new specific headwind to monitor.
on_deck.rationale: Inference KV-cache memory demand is real and growing; HBM is supply-constrained. Valuation is compelling if earnings growth materializes.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $406-$425 (50-day SMA zone) with export control clarity from next earnings or regulatory update

caution_trim.note: Avoid. 5.3% above 50-day SMA with RSI 65.7 and a reportedly pending Indonesia acquisition adding capital deployment uncertainty.
Avoid. 27% extreme extension, negative FCF yield, 56.5x forward PE at 136% premium to Semis cluster median — NVIDIA investment is priced in and then some.
Avoid. 31% extreme extension, RSI 80.2, trailing PE 106x. Meta GPU framework is a ceiling not a firm order; first shipments not until H2 2026.
Avoid. 40% extreme extension, 64x forward PE, negative FCF yield, no confirmed 18A external customers. Earnings in ~4 days add binary risk.

hold_no_action.note: Upgrades to WATCH on pullback to $391-$395 (50-day SMA). April 30 earnings — watch non-OpenAI Azure RPO as the clean demand signal, not headline Azure AI growth.
Upgrades to WATCH on pullback to $308-$319 zone. April 30 earnings are the near-term catalyst — TPU rental growth (Meta, Anthropic, Apple) is the thesis validator.
Upgrades to WATCH on pullback to $225-$226 (200-day SMA support). April 30 earnings — AWS acceleration timeline and $200B capex ROI are the key questions.
Upgrades to WATCH on 15-18% pullback to $334-$370 zone. Meta ASIC deal through 2029 is confirmed; AVGO earnings June 4 are the next catalyst.
Upgrades to WATCH on pullback to $7.51-$8.14 support with 50-day SMA hold. ADR delisting risk is a standing ceiling on conviction.

new_stocks_to_watch.name: Coherent Corp
new_stocks_to_watch.note: NVIDIA $2B photonics investment peer to LITE. S&P 500 member. Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels or if the photonics AI optics thesis shows multi-quarter demand durability beyond the LITE/COHR announcement catalysts.

new_stocks_to_watch.name: Marvell Technology
new_stocks_to_watch.note: Google reportedly in talks with Marvell for next-gen TPUs (Investor's Business Daily, April 19). Becomes watchlist-worthy if Google April 30 earnings confirm a custom silicon design partnership — that's the specific trigger.
